title: pouteria resinosa
intro: Pouteria resinosa, also known as Brazil copaiba, is a tree species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Sapotaceae. It is valued for its resin, which is extracted and used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The resin of Pouteria resinosa has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, offering health benefits such as accelerated wound healing and reduced inflammation. In herbal preparations, the resin is often used in tinctures, ointments, and salves, and is also added to traditional remedies like teas and infusions.
